campus: Rome (Faculty of Civil and Industrial Engineering)
admission_type: restricted access – pre-selection evaluation (no written entrance test)
admission_test_type: Pre-selection evaluation of curriculum and GPA; no written entrance test. International applicants evaluated on GPA (minimum 72/100 or equivalent).
application_platform: MoveIN platform for non-EU pre-selection; Infostud for admission/enrolment
access_quota_non_eu: [uncertain] – programme-specific non-EU quota not extracted; general Sapienza MoveIN rule applies (max 25 per non-EU country)
places_and_foreign_contingent: [uncertain] – exact places and non-EU contingent not found in sources checked

academic_requirements.curricular_requirements: 18 ECTS in mathematics sectors (MAT/03, MAT/05, MAT/06, MAT/07, MAT/08, MAT/09); 12 ECTS in chemistry/physics/engineering sectors (CHIM/03, CHIM/07, FIS/01 etc.); 18 ECTS in civil engineering sectors (ICAR/01-11, ICAR/17, ICAR/20). International: engineering degree or equivalent with strong mathematics and physics background.

country_specific_eligibility: [uncertain] – country-specific rules not individually extracted; see MoveIN academic requirements PDF for 2026-2027

language_requirements.note: Good written and oral communication in English is mandatory. B2 level required per programme's dedicated entry-requirements page. Acceptable proof includes TOEFL/IELTS scores or prior studies in English.

language_submission_timing: [uncertain] – at MoveIN pre-selection or at admission
italian_language_requirement: Italian not required for admission; programme taught entirely in English.
application_deadline_eu: 31 July 2026 for EU and EU-equivalent candidates (A.Y. 2026-2027) [uncertain – programme-internal deadline may differ]
application_deadline_non_eu: MoveIN pre-selection window 22 December 2025 – 15 May 2026 for non-EU candidates residing abroad requiring a visa
application_fee: €30 per application via MoveIN (max 2 per candidate per intake)
visa_preenrolment_deadline: MoveIN pre-selection by 15 May 2026; Universitaly pre-enrolment per national deadline
required_documents: Bachelor's degree certificate or enrolment certificate
For foreign qualifications: Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA statement, legalized/translated documents
document_translation_authentication: Foreign documents require official translation and legalization/apostille, plus a Declaration of Value (DoV) or CIMEA comparability statement.

entry_exam_or_test.selection: Pre-selection evaluation of curriculum, GPA, and academic background. No admission test required.

test_score_conversion_or_threshold: Minimum GPA 72/100 (or international equivalent) for international applicants

source_quotes.quote: There is no admission test - your application will be evaluated in the pre-selection process and you will get a response from us if you are eligible for admission or not.
B2 level (according to the Common European Framework) – good written and oral communication is necessary for academic success.
Minimum GPA: 72/100 (or equivalent international grading scale)

uncertainty_notes: Degree class not confirmed from course page; standard LM class for Transport Systems Engineering is likely LM-23 or LM-33 — must verify on catalogue.
2026-2027 Call not yet published; central Sapienza international deadlines (15 May, 31 July) applied.
Per-programme non-EU quota and total places not extracted.
GPA threshold of 72/100 sourced from dedicated programme site; verify against 2026-2027 call.
